data.sentence: Hi, is this Kendra? This is David Kim calling from Aegis Cloud Security support, I have case number 6977 pulled up here.
Yes, hi David, thanks for calling back. I've been waiting on this — we submitted that ticket two days ago.

I completely understand, and I apologize for the delay getting back to you. I want to make sure we get this sorted out today. So you're seeing slow backup performance with Aegis Protect — can you walk me through what you're experiencing?

Yeah, so — I mean, it's been terrible honestly. Our nightly backup jobs are taking, uh, somewhere between four and six hours to complete. They used to finish in under ninety minutes. This started maybe three weeks ago, and it's just... it's not getting better.
Four to six hours, okay, that's a significant regression. And this is consistent every night, or are there certain nights where it's worse than others?

It's pretty much every night. I mean, maybe slightly worse on weekends but honestly it's bad across the board. And look, we're a retail company — we have a lot of transaction data, inventory records, customer data — we can't have backup windows bleeding into our morning operations.
Right, yeah, that makes total sense and that's a real operational concern. Um, let me ask — what's the approximate data volume you're backing up each night? Do you have a rough figure?
It's around, uh, two to two and a half terabytes per night. That hasn't changed dramatically. I mean, we've grown a little but nothing that should cause this kind of slowdown.
Got it. And have you made any changes on your end around that time — network configuration, storage infrastructure, anything like that? Or was this kind of just one day it started being slow?
Not that I'm aware of. I mean, I had my team look into it and we didn't push any major infrastructure changes around that time. So we're kind of pointing the finger at your platform, honestly.
I hear you, and I'm not here to deflect blame — I want to find the root cause wherever it is. Let me pull up your account and look at the job logs from the last couple weeks here.
Please do. And while you're at it, I want to flag — we're running incremental backups, not full backups, so the fact that it's taking this long is extra concerning to me.
Yeah, that's a really good point, incremental jobs should absolutely not be taking that long. Okay, I'm in your account now... I can see the job history here. Hmm. Okay so I'm seeing some things.
What are you seeing? Because my team has been asking me for answers and I don't have any.

So I can see that your backup jobs are completing, but the throughput rate has dropped significantly. You're running at roughly 120 to 140 megabytes per second, whereas historically your jobs were running closer to 400 or 500. That's the bottleneck.
Okay so something changed on your end then. Because we didn't touch anything.

It could be a few things — I want to dig into this more before I point fingers anywhere. One thing I'm noticing is that your backup agent version — you're running 3.4.1, and we released 3.6.0 back in late January. There were some performance optimizations in that release, specifically around incremental change detection.
Wait — so you're saying there might be a performance issue in the version we're running and there's already a fix out?

I don't want to say definitively that's the cause yet, but it is worth flagging. There was a known issue in 3.4.x where under certain conditions the change block tracking mechanism could — um — essentially over-scan, meaning it's processing more data than it needs to.
David, that sounds like exactly what's happening. Why weren't we notified about this? This feels like something we should have gotten an email about or... I don't know, something.

That's fair feedback and I'm going to note that in the case. Customers on affected versions should have been flagged for that update and I'll look into why that didn't happen for your account. That's a process gap we need to close.

Yeah, I mean — look, I like Aegis, we've been customers for over a year and overall we've been happy. But this kind of thing is frustrating. We're a retail business, our data pipeline is critical, and three weeks of bad backups is not something I can just shrug off.

Absolutely, and I'm not asking you to shrug it off. Let's focus on getting you fixed first and then I'll make sure the right people on our side hear this feedback. So — upgrading to 3.6.0, that's the immediate step I'd recommend. Can your team do that tonight?
We can probably do it tonight or tomorrow night. What's involved in the upgrade? Is there any downtime, any risk?

It's a rolling upgrade, so there's minimal impact. The agent restarts but the restart window is typically under two minutes. You'd want to do it outside your backup window obviously, but it shouldn't cause any data issues or anything like that.
Okay. And what if we do the upgrade and it's still slow? Then what?

If you're still seeing the issue after the upgrade, the next thing I'd want to look at is your network egress path — specifically whether there's any throttling or rate limiting happening between your environment and our ingestion endpoints. I can also set you up with enhanced logging on the next backup run so we get more granular telemetry.
Okay, that's fine. I mean I'm hopeful the upgrade fixes it but I've been hopeful before with this and it hasn't gotten better on its own.

I get it. I don't want to just give you hope and disappear — here's what I'll do. I'm going to send you the upgrade instructions in writing right after this call, and I'll personally follow up with you on Monday morning to check on how the backup ran. I'll keep this case open until we confirm the performance is back to where it should be.
Monday morning works. But David, if we're still seeing the same numbers Monday, I need escalation. I can't keep going back to my team with 'we're looking into it.'

Completely fair. If the upgrade doesn't resolve it and we don't have a clear answer by Monday, I'll escalate this to our senior engineering team and loop in your account manager. You have my word on that.
Alright. I'll hold you to that. Go ahead and send me those upgrade instructions and I'll get my team moving on it tonight.
Will do. I'll have that in your inbox within the next thirty minutes. And Kendra — again, I'm sorry this dragged on as long as it did. We'll get it resolved.
Appreciate it. Talk Monday.
Talk Monday. Thanks Kendra, take care.
